rulings.Case Title: GREGORY HAMEL et al VS. 360 SIXTH STREET OWNERS ASSOCIATION, et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn For Attorneys' Fees And Litigation Expenses
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, July 17, 2014, Line 14: PLAINTIFFS GREGORY HAMEL and ERSY SHIVERS' Motion For Attorneys' Fees And Litigation Expenses. The court grants $220,000 to plaintiffs as reasonable attorney's fees. The court slightly reduces the claimed fees because several of the Morgan/Marsh billing entries are vague block bills that obscure the nature of the work performed. The court does not award plaintiff its parking and mileage litigation costs. The other claimed fees are reasonable. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: GREGORY HAMEL et al VS. 360 SIXTH STREET OWNERS ASSOCIATION, et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION To Tax Costs
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, July 17, 2014, Line 14: DEFENDANT 360 6TH STREET OWNERS ASSOCIATION'S Motion To Tax Costs. The court grants the motion to tax in part. The court grants the motion as to category 4 [expert deposition fees]. CCP 1033.5(b)(1) provides that only court ordered expert fees are recoverable. Bussey v. Affleck (1990) 225 Cal.App.3d 1162, is not persuasive authority to the contrary. See Robert L. Cloud & Assoc., Inc. v. Mikesell (1999) 69 Cal.App.4th 1141, 1154. The court grants as to category 13 [mileage] because the costs of going to and from court are not recoverable. See Ladas v. California State Auto. Assn. (1993) 19 Cal.App.4th 761, 775. The court denies as to the remaining costs. Those costs (mediation and messenger fees) were reasonably necessary to the conduct of the litigation. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: NEGINA MOHAMMAD VS. LITTLE CASEAR ENTERPRISES, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn For Order Compelling Reponses To Req For Production Of Docs Against
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Thursday, July 17, 2014, Line 1, PLAINTIFF NEGINA MOHAMMAD's Motion For Order Compelling Reponses To Request For Production Of Docs Against Pro Tem Judge Steven Stein, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Motion to compel is granted to the extent that defendant shall provide a verified response that addresses the existence/non-existence of electronic documents and produce same to the extent they exist. The defendant's supplemental response to the document categories in other respects meets statutory requirements . The holding in Regency Health Services v. Superior Court (1998) 64 Cal.App.4th 1496 regarding responsive duties by counsel and parties does not apply as that case references interrogatories. Further discovery as to the existence/non-existence of water damaged documents potentially available to be produced is best explored through a deposition of a representative of the defendant. The request for monetary sanctions is denied. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to sbs@knoxricksen.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: MANDIP DHILLON VS. LASERAWAY MEDICAL GROUP, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff Mandip Dhillon'S Motion To Quash Subpoena Re Plaintiff'S Employment History And Request For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Thursday, July 17, 2014, Line 3, Plaintiff Mandip Dhillon'S Motion To Quash Subpoena Re Plaintiff'S Employment History And Request For Sanctions Pro Tem Judge Steven Stein, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Motion to quash is granted. The defendant has failed to meet the necessary enhanced burden to show relevance applicable to employment records from previous employers. See Board of Trustees v. Superior Court (1981) 119 Cal. App. 3d 516 [174 Cal. Rptr. 160]. Sanctions in the sum of $1,000.00 are awarded as against defendant, to be paid within thirty days of the date of this Order. Sanction are awarded because of defendant's failure to engage in a meaningful "meet and confer" process after receipt of plaintiff's "meet and confer" letter which should have resulted in a significant limitation as to the scope of the subpoenas. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to sbs@knoxricksen.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: FRANKLIN H WRIGHT VS. MARRIOTT INTERNATIONAL, INC.
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Demurrer And Demurrer To Plaintiff'S Cause Of Action Against San Francisco
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, July 17, 2014, Line 7: DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S Demurrer To Plaintiff's Cause Of Action Against San Francisco. The demurrer is sustained without leave to amend. See CCP 430.10(e). Plaintiff fails to plead a viable breach of contract cause of action against defendant. Plaintiff does not allege facts showing consideration or cite authority explaining how a police department general order can constitute a binding contractual term with a member of the public. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)
